title: Test Your Agent
lede: Never launch an agent you haven't talked to. iMash gives you three ways to rehearse: a live in-browser conversation, a one-click Live Test on the node canvas, and a real phone call to your own number — then a full replay of every test in Call Logs.

steps.title: Open the Agent You Want to Test
steps.content:
Everything starts from your agent list.

  1. In the left sidebar, under Intelligence Center, click Smart Agents.
  2. Find the agent you want to test and click its name (or the green eye icon in the Action column) to open the agent editor.
💡 Testing is completely safe — nothing you say in a test call changes the agent's configuration. You can experiment as much as you like.

images.alt: Smart Agents list with agents ready to open
images.caption: Intelligence Center → Smart Agents — click an agent's name to open it.

images.alt: Smart Agents list with agents ready to open
images.caption: Intelligence Center → Smart Agents — click an agent's name to open it.

steps.title: Show the Test Agent Panel
steps.content:
In the agent editor's Form view, the test console lives in a dedicated right-hand column.

  1. Look at the toolbar at the top of the editor. Make sure Form is selected in the Form / Node switch.
  2. Click the Test Agent button in the top-right of the toolbar. It shows or hides the test column on the right side of the screen.

The right column shows the test chat with two buttons at the top:

  • Connect — starts a live voice session with your agent, right in the browser.
  • Debug — opens a live event monitor for the session (enabled once you're connected). Great for watching tool calls and transitions as they happen.

images.alt: Agent editor toolbar with the Test Agent button
images.caption: The Test Agent button sits at the far right of the editor toolbar.

images.alt: Agent editor toolbar with the Test Agent button
images.caption: The Test Agent button sits at the far right of the editor toolbar.

images.alt: Agent editor with the Test Agent panel on the right
images.caption: The editor with the Test Agent column visible on the right.

images.alt: Agent editor with the Test Agent panel on the right
images.caption: The editor with the Test Agent column visible on the right.

steps.title: Connect and Talk to Your Agent
steps.content:
This is the magic moment — a real conversation with your AI, no phone number needed.

  1. Click Connect at the top of the test panel.
  2. Your browser may ask for microphone permission — click Allow. The panel also reminds you: "You may be prompted to allow audio in your browser."
  3. Wait a moment while it shows Connecting to Agent..., then just start speaking. You'll hear the agent's greeting and voice exactly as callers will.
  4. Check the essentials: the greeting message, the voice, how it answers questions, and whether tools (like booking or transfers) trigger when they should.
⚠️ If you don't hear anything, check that your microphone isn't blocked: click the padlock/settings icon in the browser's address bar and set Microphone to Allow, then reconnect.
💡 Test sessions are real calls — they appear in Call Logs and use your balance just like production calls, so you always test the true experience.

images.alt: Test Agent panel before connecting
images.caption: Click Connect to start — the Debug button unlocks once the session is live.

images.alt: Test Agent panel before connecting
images.caption: Click Connect to start — the Debug button unlocks once the session is live.

steps.title: Type, Mute, and Watch the Live Transcript
steps.content:
While connected, the panel becomes a live transcript of the conversation — every word you say and every agent reply appears as a chat bubble in real time.

  • Type a message... — you can also chat by text: type in the box at the bottom and press Enter (or click the send button). Perfect for quiet offices or precise, repeatable test phrases.
  • Microphone button (bottom-left) — mutes or unmutes your mic mid-session.
  • Debug — opens the live event monitor so you can watch what the agent is doing behind the scenes during the call.
  • Disconnect (red) — ends the test session.
💡 The fastest tuning loop: Disconnect → edit the prompt, voice, or tools → save → Connect again. Your changes are live on the very next session — no deploy step, ever.

images.alt: Live test session with a transcript bubble and Disconnect button
images.caption: Connected: your words appear as chat bubbles, and Disconnect ends the session.

images.alt: Live test session with a transcript bubble and Disconnect button
images.caption: Connected: your words appear as chat bubbles, and Disconnect ends the session.

steps.title: Live Test From the Node Canvas
steps.content:
Building your agent in the visual Node view? You don't need to switch back to Form view to test.

  1. In the editor toolbar, switch to Node in the Form / Node switch.
  2. Find the Agent Builder bar at the bottom of the canvas and flip its toggle to Live Test.
  3. Click Start and talk to your agent, exactly like the Form-view test panel.

The bonus: while a Live Test runs, the canvas highlights the node your agent is currently on — you literally watch the conversation travel through your flow, which makes debugging transitions effortless.

💡 The AI Builder side of the same toggle is a different tool — it's an AI that edits your flow for you. Keep the toggle on Live Test when you want to talk to the agent itself.

images.alt: Agent Builder bar with the Live Test toggle and Start button
images.caption: Node view: flip to Live Test and press Start to talk to the agent on the canvas.

images.alt: Agent Builder bar with the Live Test toggle and Start button
images.caption: Node view: flip to Live Test and press Start to talk to the agent on the canvas.

steps.title: Open the Web Dialer for Phone Testing
steps.content:
Browser tests prove the brain; a phone test proves the whole pipeline — carrier audio, latency, and caller ID included.

  1. Click the round phone icon in the top-right corner of the header (visible on every page). The web dialer opens as a right-side panel.
  2. You'll see a dial pad plus four actions: Call, Text, AI Agent, and Teammate.
  • Call — places a real outbound phone call to the number you dial.
  • Text — opens a new SMS composer prefilled with the dialed number.
  • AI Agent — starts an in-browser voice call with any of your agents (the same experience as the Test Agent panel, available from anywhere in the app).
  • Teammate — rings a colleague's web dialer for an internal call.

images.alt: Web dialer panel with dial pad and Call, Text, AI Agent, Teammate buttons
images.caption: The web dialer: dial pad on top, Call / Text / AI Agent / Teammate actions below.

images.alt: Web dialer panel with dial pad and Call, Text, AI Agent, Teammate buttons
images.caption: The web dialer: dial pad on top, Call / Text / AI Agent / Teammate actions below.

steps.title: Test With a Real Phone Call to Your Own Number
steps.content:
Hear your agent exactly as your customers will — through a real carrier, on your own phone.

  1. In the web dialer, type your own mobile number on the dial pad.
  2. Click Call. The Start Call window opens.
  3. Fill in the three settings:
    • Caller ID (dial-out number) — the number the call is placed from; it's what shows on your phone's screen. Pick one of your iMash phone numbers.
    • AI Agent — the agent that joins the call. Select the agent you're testing.
    • Call mode — choose AI Mode: the AI talks and you listen (and answer as the "customer"). Human Mode is the opposite: you talk while the AI silently transcribes.
  4. Click Call. Your phone rings — answer it and have a full conversation with your agent.
⚠️ To place phone calls you need at least one phone number connected to your account. If the Caller ID list is empty, add a number first (see "Add a Phone Number" below).

images.alt: Start Call window with Caller ID, AI Agent, and Call mode options
images.caption: Start Call: pick the Caller ID, the AI Agent, and AI Mode — then hit Call.

images.alt: Start Call window with Caller ID, AI Agent, and Call mode options
images.caption: Start Call: pick the Caller ID, the AI Agent, and AI Mode — then hit Call.

steps.title: Review Every Test in Call Logs
steps.content:
Every test — browser or phone — is logged like a real call, so you can study it afterwards.

  1. In the left sidebar, under Call Center, click Call Logs.
  2. Your test calls appear at the top of the list. In-browser tests show Web in the Type column; phone tests show their direction and numbers.
  3. Use the filters (date range, Call Type, Direction, AI Agent, phone-number search) to isolate your test sessions, and the stat tiles above the table for totals like call count, duration, and cost.

images.alt: Call Logs list showing recent web test calls
images.caption: Call Logs: each test session becomes a row — Web type for in-browser tests.

images.alt: Call Logs list showing recent web test calls
images.caption: Call Logs: each test session becomes a row — Web type for in-browser tests.

steps.title: Open Call Details: Transcript, Analysis, and Cost
steps.content:
This is where you grade your agent's performance.

  1. In Call Logs, click the green eye icon at the end of a row. The Call Details panel opens.
  2. If call recording is enabled for the agent, an audio player with a waveform sits at the top — press play to relisten to the whole call.
  3. Work through the tabs:
    • Call Overview — call ID, agent name, type & direction, status, duration, and start time.
    • Transcripts — the full written conversation, turn by turn.
    • Analysis — the post-call summary and extracted results (success evaluation, structured data) if configured for the agent.
    • Call Cost — the exact cost breakdown of the call by component.
    • Observer Events — live-monitoring events, when the Observer Agent is enabled.
💡 Found something off in the transcript? That's exactly what testing is for — go back to the editor, adjust the prompt or settings, and run another test. When a full conversation reads clean end-to-end, your agent is ready for real callers.

images.alt: Call Details panel with Call Overview, Transcripts, Analysis and Call Cost tabs
images.caption: Call Details: overview, transcripts, analysis, and cost for every test call.

images.alt: Call Details panel with Call Overview, Transcripts, Analysis and Call Cost tabs
images.caption: Call Details: overview, transcripts, analysis, and cost for every test call.

nextSteps.title: What's Next?
nextSteps.description: Your agent passed the audition — now give it more power and put it in front of the world:

cards.description: Give your agent a real number so customers can call it 24/7.

cards.title: Add Tools
cards.description: Enable actions like booking appointments, transfers, and ending calls.

cards.title: Embed on Your Website
cards.description: Publish the agent as a voice/chat widget on your site.

support.title: Need Help?
support.content:
If you run into any issues or have questions:

  • 📧 Email us at support@imash.io
  • 💬 Join our Discord community
  • 📚 Check out our API Reference
  • 🎥 Watch our video tutorials

metaDescription: Test your iMash Smart Agent before launch: talk to it live in the browser, run a Live Test from the node canvas, call it on a real phone number, and review transcripts, analysis, and cost in Call Logs.
metaKeywords: test agent, imash, smart agent, live test, web dialer, test call, transcript, call logs, voice agent testing
